records.ety: [OF. agister; (L. ad) + gister to assign a lodging, fr. giste lodging, abode, F. gte, LL. gistum, gista, fr. L. jacitum, p. p. of jacēre to lie: cf. LL. agistare, adgistare. See Gist.]

defs: To take to graze or pasture, at a certain sum; -- used originally of the feeding of cattle in the king's forests, and collecting the money for the same.
